column_name: MV Hondius Andes Hantavirus Research Data Pack

value: The uploaded Yang et al. paper used migration In/Out terms in modified SEIR; this workbook adapts that logic to ship-to-flight outflows.

Dataset description: aggregated epidemiological/geographic data from WHO, ECDC and French Ministry; includes ship route, case evolution, evacuations, deaths, repatriated nationals; CSV updated 2026-05-11
MV Hondius departed; itinerary across South Atlantic; 147 individuals reported by WHO (88 passengers + 59 crew).
Case 1 developed fever, headache and mild diarrhoea while on board.
Case 1 developed respiratory distress and died on board.
Itinerary included Tristan da Cunha; later a British national case was supported/monitored there according to UKHSA updates.
Body of case 1 removed; Case 2 went ashore with GI symptoms; WHO reports contact tracing of disembarked passengers later ongoing.
Case 2 deteriorated during flight to Johannesburg; contact tracing for passengers on the flight was initiated.
Case 2 died on arrival at emergency department; later PCR-confirmed hantavirus infection.
Case 3 medically evacuated to South Africa and admitted to ICU.
Case 4 symptom onset: fever and malaise; later died on 2 May.
WHO notified of cluster; South Africa lab confirmed hantavirus in critically ill patient; Case 4 died same day.
Additional death reported after WHO notification; investigations and coordination escalated.
WHO: 7 cases total, 2 lab-confirmed and 5 suspected, including 3 deaths; vessel moored off Cabo Verde.
Two medical evacuation flights from Cabo Verde carried two symptomatic confirmed patients and one previously suspected case, landing in the Netherlands on 6/7 May.
Ship left Cabo Verde heading to Canary Islands, Spain, where disembarkation was planned.
